Cookbook | Recipes | Ingredients | Equipment | Techniques | Cookbook Disambiguation Pages | Ingredients  | Chili Pepper

A **jalapeño** or **cuaresmeño**[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) is a moderately hot variety of chili pepper.

## Characteristics

[edit | edit source]

Jalapeños are a medium-size chile, generally around 2–5 inches (5–13 cm) long and 1 inch (2.5 cm) across.[[2]](#cite_note-:5-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4) The shape is smoothly tapered and blunt.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5) Like many other chiles, they are green while immature before ripening to red.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)[[2]](#cite_note-:5-2)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7) The flavor of the green stage is somewhat vegetal, becoming a little sweeter when ripe and red.[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6) The pepper is only moderately hot , ranging between 2,500 and 8,000 Scoville units.[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7) Jalapeños may have tiny pale cracks across the skins, which are essentially stretch marks.[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4) The flesh is thick and crunchy.[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4)

When dried and smoked, jalapeños become chipotles.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7) Jalapeños may also be canned.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7)

## Selection and storage

[edit | edit source]

It is common to harvest jalapeños when immature and green.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7) Whether green or red, fresh jalapeños should be firm and glossy with no wrinkles.[[2]](#cite_note-:5-2) They should be kept, unwashed and wrapped in paper towels, in the fridge for up to a few weeks.[[2]](#cite_note-:5-2)

## Use

[edit | edit source]

Jalapeños are common in the cuisines of Mexico and the southwestern United States.[[5]](#cite_note-:4-5)[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7) Their thickness and crunchy texture lend them to chunky preparations like salsa.[[4]](#cite_note-:3-4) They can be stuffed, pickled, blackened and skinned, and incorporated into a wide variety of cooked and raw preparations.[[2]](#cite_note-:5-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)
